channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to federal government's payment system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Treasury Department's Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government's payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk's Department of Governme
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Wisconsin's spring primary
item.description: Wisconsin Superintendent of Public Instruction Jill Underly seeks a second four-year term in Tuesday’s spring primary.
item.dc:creator: Robert Yoon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Guarded optimism in India as Trump and Modi outline plans to deepen defense partnership
item.description: SRINAGAR, India (AP) — There was guarded optimism among military experts in India as Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump outlined plans to ramp up sales of defense systems to New Delhi, including F-35 stealth fighter jets, to deepen the U.S.-India strategic relationship.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is Elon Musk overreaching with DOGE? Lawmakers debate
item.description: Is Elon Musk, head of DOGE, being transparent? Reps. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., and Byron Donalds, R-Fla., debate on NewsNation's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speedy cross-border work by Minnesota’s MMIR Office credited with quick return of First Nations woman to Canada
item.description: Feb. 14 has dual significance. It’s Valentine’s Day, but also a day of remembrance to honor missing and murdered Indigenous relatives. It’s also a day to celebrate triumphs.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Senate Majority Leader Thune says this is the reason why he and Trump are working well together
item.description: GOP Senate Majority Leader John Thune talks exclusively with Fox News Digital about dealing with President Donald Trump and his working relationship with former leader Mitch McConnell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top moments from Linda McMahon's confirmation hearing
item.description: Linda McMahon's Senate confirmation hearing Thursday featured protests, DEI debates, discussions on trans athletes and scrutiny over DOGE spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taiwan pledges to communicate and invest more in the US after Trump tariff threats
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan President Lai Ching-te said Friday he would communicate more with the U.S. over President Donald Trump’s concerns over the chip industry and invest more in the U.S, hours after Trump ordered reciprocal tariffs on trade partners.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders temporary reversal of Trump admin’s freeze on foreign aid
item.description: A federal judge late Thursday issued an order compelling the Trump administration to lift its three-week funding freeze on U.S. foreign aid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida passes immigration bill, repealing in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.description: The third and final day of the special session concluded with the passage of an immigration bill, one that sparked significant debate among lawmakers in both the House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Trump Setting Records
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House blocks AP reporter from Trump-Modi news conference because of Gulf of Mexico fight
item.description: The White House blocked an Associated Press journalist from covering a news conference with two major world leaders Thursday, upping the stakes in a disagreement between the news agency and the Trump administration over AP's style decision to stick with the name “Gulf of Mexico” for the body of wate
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance visits the Dachau concentration camp memorial with Holocaust survivor before Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: DACHAU, Germany (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance visited the Dachau concentration camp memorial on Thursday, walking the solemn halls with a group that included a Holocaust survivor once held at the site of so much suffering and mass murder.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. sworn in as Health and Human Services secretary
item.description: Hours after being confirmed in the Senate, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. was sworn in as Health and Human Services secretary alongside his family in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seems to question well-known part of Sen. Mitch McConnell's life
item.description: President Donald Trump seemed to question a well-documented part of Sen. Mitch McConnell's life — his childhood battle with polio — after the Kentucky Republican opposed vaccine skeptic Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s confirmation Thursday as the nation's top health official.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: A look at who might run for Sen. Tina Smith’s seat in the Senate
item.description: With Sen. Tina Smith’s announcement that she will not seek reelection in 2026, politicians on both sides of the aisle are eying her seat. Here are some of those who say they plan to run — or are at least considering it.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Minnesotans, other politicians react to Sen. Tina Smith's decision not to run again
item.description: Minnesota politicians from both sides of the aisle have weighed in on the news that U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, who has served in the Senate since 2018, is retiring after next year.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Johnson leaves door open to full-year funding stopgap
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) didn’t rule out a full-year stopgap to keep the government funded through the end of the fiscal year, as lawmakers struggle to strike a bipartisan deal. Pressed by reporters on the chances of a full-year stopgap, Johnson left the door open on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. confirmed as Health and Human Services secretary
item.description: Kennedy confirmed in party-line vote, with final tally of 58-42. McConnell was only Republican lawmaker to vote no.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Texas now has more top-tier research universities than any other state, report finds
item.description: The designations come at a tumultuous time for universities as funding could be cut off and efforts to end tenure continue.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Attorney general: US sues New York officials over immigration enforcement
item.description: Bondi said the lawsuit takes aim at a "green light law" that allows people who are living in the country illegally to obtain driver's licenses in New York.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation
